title: Review and setlist: Leslie Odom Jr. brings Broadway to the Boston Pops
author: Shannon Murphy
bullets: Leslie Odom Jr. performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on May 22, 2026, for their spring season.
The program included pieces by Rodgers, Bernstein, Ellington, and Gould, highlighting American musical heritage.
Odom Jr. performed songs from 'Hamilton' and classics like 'Fly Me To The Moon' with a theatrical flair.

title: Chelsea man facing slew of assault charges for alleged violence against young children
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Kener Bautista-Zepeda, 19, faces multiple assault charges on allegations of harming two young children in Chelsea.
He allegedly burned a child with a cigarette lighter in March 2025 and used a heated fork on another in April 2026.
Bautista-Zepeda pleaded not guilty and is held without bail pending a dangerousness hearing on May 28.

title: Concord man, 73, convicted of raping 20-year-old in Sandwich restaurant bathroom in 2023
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Elie Jammal, 73, of Concord was convicted for the rape and indecent assault of a 20-year-old in Sandwich, 2023.
Jammal was sentenced to 13 ½ to 15 years in prison and 5 years probation by Barnstable County court.
The court highlighted Jammal's "shocking sense of entitlement" as his motivations, dismissing intoxication as a factor.

title: Newton Church, founded in 1893, is becoming condos
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Newton's Church of the Open Word, built in 1893, will be converted into six condos with a new 26-unit building added.
Developer Habitech Communities bought the church property for $4 million, with plans to preserve the sanctuary and demolish the parish house.
Similar projects are underway as former churches in Greater Boston are converted into residential spaces due to shrinking congregations.

title: 5 players the Celtics could target this offseason outside of Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Kawhi Leonard could be a potential trade target for the Boston Celtics, depending on his availability from the Clippers.
LeBron James, an unrestricted free agent, could fit well with Boston's lineup if he decides to leave Los Angeles.
John Collins might add forward depth to the Celtics, having averaged 13 points and five rebounds per game last season.

title: 2 local college baseball programs advance to NCAA World Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Bentley University advances to the NCAA Division II World Series after a 3-2 victory over Molloy University in Waltham.
Endicott College progresses to the Division III championship round for the fourth time by defeating Salve Regina University.
Bentley's Stan DeMartinis III scored the winning run, while Endicott's AJ Hamm hit a home run in their respective victories.

title: Celtics reportedly plan to work out UConn basketball star ahead of NBA Draft
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Celtics plan a pre-draft workout for UConn's Alex Karaban, scheduled for next week.
Karaban, ranked No. 32 by ESPN, averaged 12.5 points and 5.0 rebounds in college.
Karaban, 23, is UConn's all-time leader in games, starts, minutes, and three-pointers made.

title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway on May 22, incorporating jazz and pop into his setlist.
Puth's concert emphasized musicianship, with improvised riffs, strong vocal control, and stripped-down visuals enhancing the performance.
Backup vocalists and opening acts Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey supported Puth, elevating the concert's musical depth and engagement.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers performed 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full at The Cabot in Beverly on May 22, 2026.
Jakob Dylan channeled Tom Petty by following their album with a performance of Petty's 'Long After Dark'.
The concert included an encore with The Wallflowers covering Petty classics 'Refugee' and 'The Waiting'.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston lost 1,338 residents last year, marking the state's largest population decline according to the latest U.S. Census data.
Everett and Worcester saw the largest population increases in Massachusetts, gaining 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively.
High housing costs and decreased immigration are cited as factors for Boston's population decline, with Massachusetts growing by 15,500 residents.
